process_ownership.runner: launcher identity + outer kill-on-close job + raw streams + exit + closure
process_ownership.launcher: controller identity + inner kill-on-close job + raw streams + exit + post-controller audit
process_ownership.controller: sentinel identity + bounded kill-on-close job + READY/release + raw streams
process_ownership.windows_console_hosts: at most one exact %WINDIR%\System32\conhost.exe descendant directly parented by each expected sealed QA role is allowed; each remains recorded in descendant inventories but is excluded from QA-role counts

journal_schema: append-only JSONL; sequence, kind, native_tick, payload, previous_sha256, record_sha256; WriteThrough + Flush(true) + exact readback before assertion

preseal_repair_history.note: Windows PowerShell Start-Process first rejected duplicate case-variant Path/PATH keys before csc started. A first QP_DRYRUN then proved that direct redirected runner launch creates a runner-owned conhost; that failing run is preserved. The cmd wrapper fixed ownership without changing the runner invocation arguments. The first complete three-test attempt then exposed cmd-normalized command-line separator whitespace in the smoke binding. The component contract was regenerated to bind the exact observed command line, with no source or component-byte change, and all three mandatory tests were rerun fresh before sealing.

native_tick_comparison_sites: SentinelExercise.Run READY plus exit within one total handshake deadline
ControllerRole LIVE target<=actual<=target+250 and strict actual increase

authorized_change_set: runner-only QP_POWER_INPUT_SMOKE using production PowerAndInput path
